name: Kentucky Turkey and Bacon Brochettes
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse turkey with cold water and pat dry. Cut each thigh into 8 equal pieces and season with salt.
2. Place the turkey pieces in a bowl with the marinade and toss well. Cover and marinate for 2 hours at room temperature, or up to 48 hours refrigerated.
3. Prepare a hot fire. Cut bacon strips in half crosswise and lay flat on a clean work surface.
4. Lift each piece of turkey from the marinade and place one piece on each bacon slice. Wrap bacon around the turkey meat and thread 4 pieces onto each of 4 oiled metal skewers, securing them well.
5. Place brochettes on an oiled grill set about 6 inches from the coals. Grill the brochettes, turning occasionally and basting with reserved marinade until the bacon is well browned and the turkey is cooked through, about 10 minutes.
KENTUCKY MARINADE: Process all ingredients in a blender or food processor until thoroughly combined. The marinade can be stored in a covered jar in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
